news.title: {ticker} Announces Record Q3 PAT; Board Approves ₹5,000 Cr Buyback
news.content: {ticker} reported a 28% YoY jump in net profit to ₹18,500 Cr for Q3 FY26, beating Street estimates by 12%. The board approved a ₹5,000 Cr share buyback at ₹3,200 per share — a 15% premium to the current market price. EBITDA margins expanded 180 bps to 24.3%. Management guided for sustained double-digit growth driven by capacity additions and new product launches in the premium segment.

news.title: {ticker} Bags ₹12,000 Cr Government Contract; FII Buying Surges
news.content: {ticker} secured a five-year ₹12,000 Cr infrastructure contract from NHAI, the largest order in the company's history. Simultaneously, FII net buying in the stock crossed ₹2,200 Cr over the past five sessions — the highest monthly inflow in four years. Three brokerages upgraded the stock to 'Buy' with revised price targets implying 22–30% upside.

news.title: {ticker} Q3 FY26 Revenue Preview: Analysts Expect 25% Growth
news.content: Ahead of {ticker}'s earnings on Monday, consensus estimates point to a 25% topline growth driven by strong domestic demand and export momentum. The stock has outperformed Nifty 50 by 18% in the last six months. Technical analysts note a golden cross on the daily chart — SMA50 crossing above SMA200 — historically a strong bullish signal for this counter.

news.title: {ticker} Under SEBI Scanner for Insider Trading; Stock Tanks 8%
news.content: SEBI has initiated an inquiry into suspected insider trading at {ticker} ahead of a major acquisition announcement last quarter. The regulator issued notices to 12 entities believed to have traded ahead of the board disclosure. The stock fell 8.3% intraday on heavy volume — the steepest single-day decline in 18 months. Legal experts estimate potential penalties could reach ₹500 Cr if violations are proven.

news.title: {ticker} Cuts FY26 Revenue Guidance by 12% Citing Margin Pressure
news.content: {ticker} issued a profit warning, revising FY26 revenue guidance downward by 12% to ₹85,000 Cr from ₹97,000 Cr earlier. Rising raw material costs and rupee depreciation are squeezing operating margins, which are now expected to contract by 200–250 bps. Two FII custodians have begun reducing their stakes, with combined net selling exceeding ₹1,800 Cr in the past week.

news.title: Goldman Sachs Downgrades {ticker} to Sell; Cuts Target by 25%
news.content: Goldman Sachs has downgraded {ticker} from 'Neutral' to 'Sell', slashing its 12-month price target by 25% to ₹2,100. The note cites deteriorating cash conversion, higher-than-expected capex, and intensifying competition from Chinese imports. Emkay and Nuvama have followed with 'Reduce' ratings. The stock is now trading near its 52-week low with RSI approaching oversold territory at 31.
